vocabulary.definition_ar: اختبار تجريبي
vocabulary.example_sentence: The company invited users for beta testing of the new app.

vocabulary.definition_ar: القدرة على نقل البيانات بين الأجهزة دون كابلات مادية
vocabulary.example_sentence: Wireless connectivity allows the headset to move freely.

vocabulary.definition_ar: جعل شيء يبدو أكبر على الشاشة من خلال التركيز عليه عن قرب أكثر
vocabulary.example_sentence: Zoom in on the image to see the small details more clearly.

vocabulary.definition_ar: المرحلة الأكثر تقدماً أو ابتكاراً في التطوير في مجال معين
vocabulary.example_sentence: This company develops cutting-edge technology for medical research.

vocabulary.definition_ar: الوصول في النهاية إلى مكان أو حالة أو موقف معين، غالبًا بشكل غير متوقع
vocabulary.example_sentence: Many people end up spending hours in virtual worlds without realizing it.

vocabulary.definition_ar: حفظ الملفات الرقمية على خوادم الإنترنت بدلاً من الأجهزة المحلية
vocabulary.example_sentence: Cloud storage allows you to access your files from anywhere.

vocabulary.definition_ar: تتبع العين
vocabulary.example_sentence: Eye tracking helps the headset know where you are looking.

vocabulary.definition_ar: توأم رقمي
vocabulary.example_sentence: Engineers use a digital twin to test designs before building.

vocabulary.definition_ar: تقنية تُصيّر التفاصيل فقط حيث تُركّز العين لتوفير قدرة المعالجة
vocabulary.example_sentence: Foveated rendering greatly reduces the computing power VR headsets need.

vocabulary.definition_ar: تشغيل أو تنفيذ برنامج أو أمر حاسوبي
vocabulary.example_sentence: The system will execute the program automatically at startup.

vocabulary.definition_ar: فهم أو حل شيء ما بعد التفكير فيه
vocabulary.example_sentence: I couldn't figure out how to use the new software.

vocabulary.definition_ar: جهاز قابل للارتداء يُحاكي الإحساس بلمس الأشياء الافتراضية
vocabulary.example_sentence: The haptic glove allowed the surgeon to practice delicate operations.

vocabulary.definition_ar: التحكم بالإيماءات
vocabulary.example_sentence: Gesture control lets you interact with VR without controllers.

vocabulary.definition_ar: أسلوب تعليمي يستخدم الواقع الافتراضي لممارسة المهارات في سيناريوهات واقعية
vocabulary.example_sentence: Firefighters use immersive training to prepare for dangerous situations.

vocabulary.definition_ar: ملابس يمكن ارتداؤها تُرسل إحساسات لمسية لمن يرتديها
vocabulary.example_sentence: The haptic suit lets players feel every virtual impact.

vocabulary.definition_ar: فضاء افتراضي مشترك يُنشئه الإنترنت وتكنولوجيا الواقع الافتراضي
vocabulary.example_sentence: Some companies hold business meetings inside the metaverse.

vocabulary.definition_ar: أسلوب سرد يضع الجمهور داخل القصة باستخدام التكنولوجيا
vocabulary.example_sentence: Immersive storytelling allows viewers to experience history firsthand.

vocabulary.definition_ar: التحول البصري بين المقدمة والخلفية مما يُولّد إحساساً بالعمق
vocabulary.example_sentence: The parallax effect in this VR scene made objects feel very close.

vocabulary.definition_ar: قائمة من الخيارات أو الأوامر تظهر على الشاشة ليختار منها المستخدم
vocabulary.example_sentence: Go to the settings menu to adjust the display brightness.

vocabulary.definition_ar: مجموعة نقاط بيانات في الفضاء تمثل جسماً أو مشهداً ثلاثي الأبعاد
vocabulary.example_sentence: The scanner created a point cloud of the ancient ruins in minutes.

vocabulary.definition_ar: سلسلة الخطوات التي يتبعها الحاسوب لإنتاج صورة نهائية
vocabulary.example_sentence: Optimising the rendering pipeline cut loading times by half.

vocabulary.definition_ar: واقع مختلط
vocabulary.example_sentence: Mixed reality allows virtual objects to interact with real ones.

vocabulary.definition_ar: حالة مشابهة لدوار الحركة تسببها تجارب الواقع الافتراضي
vocabulary.example_sentence: Reducing simulation sickness is a top priority for VR designers.

vocabulary.definition_ar: واقعي ضوئياً
vocabulary.example_sentence: The game graphics are so photorealistic that they seem real.

vocabulary.definition_ar: مجموعة من القواعد التي تحكم كيفية نقل البيانات بين الأجهزة
vocabulary.example_sentence: Internet protocols ensure that data reaches its destination correctly.

vocabulary.definition_ar: حوسبة مكانية
vocabulary.example_sentence: Spatial computing lets users interact with digital objects in space.

vocabulary.definition_ar: يسبب أو يشمل تغييراً كاملاً أو جذرياً
vocabulary.example_sentence: Virtual reality technology has revolutionary potential for education and training.

vocabulary.definition_ar: إرسال أو استقبال البيانات بشكل مستمر عبر الإنترنت في الوقت الفعلي
vocabulary.example_sentence: Video streaming quality depends on your internet connection speed.

vocabulary.definition_ar: نظام تجاري لتبادل القيمة داخل بيئة رقمية
vocabulary.example_sentence: Players earn and spend real money within the game's virtual economy.

vocabulary.definition_ar: تقنية صوتية تُوهم بسماع ثلاثي الأبعاد
vocabulary.example_sentence: Spatial audio made the virtual concert feel completely real.

vocabulary.definition_ar: حجمي
vocabulary.example_sentence: Volumetric video captures objects from every angle.

vocabulary.definition_ar: عملية إنشاء خريطة رقمية ثلاثية الأبعاد لفضاء مادي
vocabulary.example_sentence: Spatial mapping lets your headset understand the furniture in your room.

vocabulary.definition_ar: حضور عن بُعد
vocabulary.example_sentence: Telepresence robots allow doctors to examine patients remotely.

vocabulary.definition_ar: الشعور بالوجود الفعلي في بيئة مُنشأة رقمياً
vocabulary.example_sentence: Strong virtual presence makes users forget they are wearing a headset.

vocabulary.definition_ar: نظام ينشئ صوراً تبدو طافية في فضاء ثلاثي الأبعاد حقيقي
vocabulary.example_sentence: The scientist used a volumetric display to show the molecule structure.

paragraphs: Virtual Reality technology has *evolved* from a science fiction dream into a powerful tool that is *reshaping* industries across the globe. What was once considered an *exclusive* technology for gaming enthusiasts has now become an *integral* part of modern education, healthcare, and professional training. According to recent studies, the global VR market is expected to reach $87 billion by 2030, demonstrating how this *innovative* technology has *caught on* with both businesses and consumers worldwide.

In educational institutions, VR applications are *revolutionizing* traditional learning methods by creating *immersive* experiences that were previously impossible to achieve. Students at King Abdulaziz University in Jeddah, for example, can now explore ancient civilizations or travel through the human circulatory system without leaving their classroom. Medical students *engage with* virtual patients to practice complex surgical procedures, while engineering students can *manipulate* three-dimensional models of machinery. Research indicates that students retain 75% of information learned through VR experiences, compared to only 10% from traditional reading methods. This *dramatic* improvement in learning outcomes has led educators to *embrace* VR technology as a *supplement* to conventional teaching approaches.

The healthcare sector has also witnessed remarkable transformations through VR implementation. Hospitals in Riyadh and other major cities are using virtual reality to help patients manage pain and anxiety during medical procedures. Therapists *employ* VR environments to treat patients with phobias, post-traumatic stress, and other psychological conditions by gradually exposing them to controlled virtual scenarios. Furthermore, surgeons can now rehearse complex operations multiple times in virtual environments before performing actual procedures, significantly reducing medical errors and improving patient safety.

Despite these promising developments, experts acknowledge that VR technology still faces certain limitations that need to be addressed. The high cost of equipment remains a *barrier* for many institutions, particularly in developing regions. Additionally, some users experience motion sickness or eye strain during extended VR sessions. However, technology companies are continuously working to overcome these challenges by developing more affordable devices and improving user comfort. As VR technology becomes more accessible and sophisticated, it is expected to *transform* countless aspects of our daily lives, from how we learn and work to how we connect with others across vast distances.

Dr. Sarah Al-Mahmoud carefully adjusts her VR headset as she prepares to *conduct* a complex heart surgery—virtually. The Riyadh-based cardiac surgeon is participating in an innovative training program that allows medical professionals to *rehearse* intricate procedures before entering the operating room. This groundbreaking approach represents how virtual reality is *transforming* healthcare delivery across the Middle East and beyond.

Medical institutions worldwide have begun to *embrace* VR technology as a powerful educational tool. At King Abdulaziz University Hospital in Jeddah, students can now *immerse* themselves in three-dimensional anatomical models, exploring the human body in ways that traditional textbooks could never *facilitate*. According to recent research conducted by Stanford Medical School, surgical residents who trained using VR simulations showed 230% better performance compared to those who relied solely on conventional methods. These *compelling* statistics have prompted healthcare administrators to *allocate* substantial budgets toward virtual reality integration.

Beyond training applications, VR technology is proving *invaluable* for patient treatment and therapy. Burn victims at specialized centers in Dubai and Riyadh have reported significantly reduced pain levels when using VR distraction therapy during wound care procedures. The immersive experiences *transport* patients to peaceful environments—perhaps walking through virtual gardens or exploring coral reefs—effectively helping them *cope* with intense discomfort. Dr. Ahmed Hassan, who *specializes* in pain management at a leading Abu Dhabi hospital, explains that patients often *lose track* of time during VR sessions, allowing medical staff to complete necessary treatments with minimal patient distress.

The *implementation* of virtual reality in healthcare extends to mental health services as well. Therapists across the Gulf region are utilizing VR environments to help patients confront phobias, manage anxiety disorders, and process traumatic experiences in controlled, safe settings. As this technology continues to evolve, medical experts predict that virtual consultations, remote surgical guidance, and AI-powered diagnostic tools will become standard practice. The integration of VR in healthcare represents not merely a technological advancement, but a fundamental shift toward more compassionate, effective patient care that respects both medical precision and human dignity.

sections.content_ar: الجمل الوصلية تعطي معلومات إضافية عن شخص أو شيء. نستخدم who للأشخاص، which للأشياء، و that للاثنين معاً
sections.content_en:
Relative clauses give us more information about a person or thing. We use relative pronouns to connect the extra information to the main sentence.

• Use who for people
• Use which for things/objects
• Use that for both people and things (more informal)

The relative clause comes directly after the noun it describes. It helps us identify which person or thing we're talking about without starting a new sentence.

sections.content: Main clause + relative pronoun (who/which/that) + additional information

items.sentence: The developer who created this VR game is very talented.
items.translation_ar: المطور الذي أنشأ هذه اللعبة الافتراضية موهوب جداً

items.sentence: The VR headset which I bought yesterday is amazing.
items.translation_ar: نظارة الواقع الافتراضي التي اشتريتها أمس رائعة

items.sentence: This is the virtual world that millions of users explore daily.
items.highlight: that millions of users explore daily
items.translation_ar: هذا هو العالم الافتراضي الذي يستكشفه ملايين المستخدمين يومياً

items.wrong: The person which designed the VR system
items.correct: The person who designed the VR system

items.wrong: The VR game who I played
items.correct: The VR game which/that I played
items.explanation_ar: استخدم which أو that للأشياء، ليس who

items.wrong: The user that he loves VR technology
items.correct: The user who loves VR technology
items.explanation_ar: لا تكرر الضمير بعد الضمير الوصلي
